rationale: Both correctly identify the #4527 regression (non-async-chunk branch nulling the inter-stage payload) and that PR #4792 already fixes it in the 0.24 rebase, matching the thread resolution. X is grounded almost word-for-word in the exact code comment shown in the ground truth diff, and delivers a clean maintainer-style answer with concrete next steps (upgrade/cherry-pick/workaround) plus a note dismissing red herrings in the reporter's env. Y reaches the same correct diagnosis and even cites extra NPU-runner files, but the output is a raw, unpolished internal 'run report' full of duplicated JSON/state-dump scaffolding rather than a coherent answer to the reporter, and it never gives explicit actionable guidance — hurting completeness and usability despite comparable technical accuracy.
